nodes.name: action_executor.py
nodes.summary: Translates AI-generated action plan steps into safe shell commands, executes them with timeout and safety checks, and logs results to a persistent JSON execution log.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses dataclass-style StepResult for structured return values; employs regex-based command allowlisting (is_safe) to guard against arbitrary shell execution.

nodes.name: StepResult
nodes.summary: Data container capturing the outcome of a single executed action step, including command text, stdout/stderr, return code, success flag, and optional skip reason.

nodes.name: map_step_to_command
nodes.summary: Maps a natural-language action step string to a concrete shell command string using regex-based pattern matching against a command builder registry.

nodes.name: is_safe
nodes.summary: Validates a shell command string against a regex allowlist to prevent execution of dangerous or unexpected commands.

nodes.name: run_command
nodes.summary: Executes a shell command via subprocess with a configurable timeout and returns stdout, stderr, and return code.

nodes.name: execute_plan
nodes.summary: Iterates an ordered action plan, maps each step to a command, applies safety checks, optionally runs in dry-run mode, executes commands, and aggregates StepResult outcomes with configurable stop-on-failure behavior.

nodes.name: _log_execution
nodes.summary: Serializes an action plan's execution results into a JSON log file, appending to existing entries to maintain a persistent audit trail of all plan executions.

nodes.name: execution_summary
nodes.summary: Formats a list of StepResult objects into a human-readable summary string showing per-step status icons and labels.

nodes.name: notion_reporter.py
nodes.summary: Posts structured incident reports and think-tank diagnostic results to a Notion database page via the Notion REST API, formatting action plans, execution summaries, and escalation details as rich-text blocks.

nodes.name: post_incident
nodes.summary: Constructs a Notion page payload with incident details (server name, fault type, verdict, action plan, execution summary, escalation) and POSTs it to the configured Notion database via the REST API.

nodes.name: report_think_tank_result
nodes.summary: Formats think-tank execution results into a Markdown-style execution summary string and delegates to post_incident to write the full incident page to Notion.

nodes.name: test_pipeline.py
nodes.summary: End-to-end integration test harness that exercises the full FusionAL watchdog pipeline — fault detection, think-tank reasoning, action plan execution, and Notion reporting — using a mock FaultEvent and a stubbed think-tank response.

nodes.name: mock_think_tank
nodes.summary: Creates a deterministic ThinkTankResult stub for a given fault, providing canned verdict, action plan, issues, and escalation data for use in integration tests without hitting the real Claude API.

nodes.name: run_test
nodes.summary: Drives the full test sequence: creates a mock FaultEvent, invokes the think-tank (real or mocked), runs the action executor against the result, and calls the Notion reporter — printing detailed status at each stage.

nodes.name: think_tank_trigger.py
nodes.summary: Implements a multi-agent Claude-powered think-tank that analyzes infrastructure faults through role negotiation, parallel diagnosis, and observer synthesis phases to produce a structured remediation verdict and action plan.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses asyncio.gather for parallel Claude API calls across multiple diagnostic roles; employs extended thinking budgets (ReasoningPreset) for deeper analysis.

nodes.name: ReasoningPreset
nodes.summary: Enum-like configuration class defining thinking budget tiers (FAST, BALANCED, DEEP) for Claude API extended thinking mode.

nodes.name: FaultEvent
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing a detected infrastructure fault event, carrying server name, fault type, detail string, trigger reason, and ISO timestamp.

nodes.name: ThinkTankResult
nodes.summary: Dataclass holding the full output of a think-tank session: verdict, action plan steps, issues list, escalation flag, escalation reason, raw per-role diagnoses, and timestamp.

nodes.name: _call_claude
nodes.summary: Wraps the Anthropic messages.create API call with optional extended thinking support, concatenating text blocks from the response into a single string.

nodes.name: run_think_tank
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the three-phase think-tank pipeline: (1) parallel role negotiation to assign diagnostic perspectives, (2) parallel per-role diagnosis generation, (3) observer synthesis to produce a final ThinkTankResult — then optionally executes the action plan and reports to Notion.

nodes.name: _log_result
nodes.summary: Persists a ThinkTankResult alongside the triggering FaultEvent to a JSON log file, appending to existing entries.

nodes.name: trigger
nodes.summary: Public entry point that wraps a fault event into a FaultEvent dataclass and synchronously runs the full think-tank pipeline via asyncio.run.

nodes.name: watchdog.py
nodes.summary: Continuous health-monitoring daemon that polls configured MCP server HTTP health endpoints, logs faults to a JSON fault log, attempts automatic Docker container restarts within rate limits, and fires the think-tank analysis pipeline asynchronously on unrecoverable failures.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses threading.Thread to fire think-tank analysis non-blocking alongside the main polling loop; tracks restart timestamps in a defaultdict to enforce per-server cooldown windows.

nodes.name: log_fault
nodes.summary: Records a server fault event (type, detail, action taken) with a UTC timestamp to a persistent JSON fault log file.

nodes.name: check_health
nodes.summary: Issues an HTTP GET to a server's health endpoint and returns True if the response is 200 OK within the configured timeout, returning False on any connection error or non-200 status.

nodes.name: can_restart
nodes.summary: Checks whether a server is eligible for an automatic restart by enforcing a maximum restart count within a sliding time window from the restart tracker.

nodes.name: restart_container
nodes.summary: Executes `docker restart` for a server's container, waits for it to become healthy, records the restart timestamp, and returns success or failure status.

nodes.name: handle_fault
nodes.summary: Fault response coordinator that decides whether to restart a container or escalate by logging the fault and firing the think-tank, based on restart eligibility and restart success.

nodes.name: _fire_think_tank
nodes.summary: Launches the think-tank trigger in a background daemon thread so fault analysis runs non-blocking alongside the watchdog polling loop.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Main watchdog polling loop that iterates over configured servers, checks each one's health, and dispatches handle_fault on unhealthy servers before sleeping for the configured interval.
